# School Stack

## The complete operating system for microschools.

---

## What We Are

School Stack is a software company that builds and sells school management platforms to microschool and small private school owners. Every feature in the platform was built inside a real, operating microschool — Sage Field in Texas — and proven there before being offered to anyone else.

The microschool market is one of the fastest-growing segments in American education: $2.7 billion in 2024, projected to reach $9.6 billion by 2033. Between 750,000 and 2.1 million students are currently enrolled in microschools nationwide. The software serving them is fragmented, outdated, or built for the wrong audience entirely.

School Stack is the only platform in this market that covers the full operational journey — from the first time a parent sees your school's website, through their application, their signed contract, their first tuition payment, and into daily classroom operations — in a single unified system, built specifically for how microschools actually work.

---

## The Problem We Solve

Microschool founders are brilliant operators who built something special. But they're running their schools across five to ten disconnected tools — Google Forms for enrollment, Venmo for payments, Gmail threads for parent communication, PDFs for contracts, Wix for a website, Excel for budget. Every tool is a context switch. Every handoff between tools is a chance for something to fall through.

The software alternatives that exist are either built for large school districts (expensive, overwhelming, wrong assumptions), built for childcare and daycare (Brightwheel, Kangarootime), or too lightweight to run a real school operation (Spark, Omella). Nothing was designed for the microschool operator managing 15–80 students, running every department alone or with a tiny team, and needing software that works like a natural extension of how they already think about their school.

That is the gap School Stack owns.

---

## What We Sell

A fully branded, white-labeled school management platform — configured with each customer's school name, domain, colors, and programs. Not a template. Not a DIY builder. A complete system handed to them ready to use, with concierge onboarding.

Customers choose a Stack based on their school's size and needs, then add custom features on top whenever they need something specific.

### Public Website & Marketing

A complete, purpose-built school website — not Wix, not a template, every section designed for school marketing:

- Dedicated pages per program with curriculum breakdowns, image galleries, and daily schedules

Every touchpoint to capture a prospective family before they leave:

### Enrollment System

Two application pathways to match different family comfort levels:

Full application (5 steps): child info, parent/guardian info, health and medical info, emergency contacts, signatures and agreements — with partial save and resume so families don't lose progress.

Quick apply (4 steps): abbreviated version for lower-friction signups.

Both feed into the same admin pipeline. Enrollment codes (discount/promo) are supported. Parents track their application status from their own dashboard.

## Competitive Landscape

### Who exists and why they fall short

**SchoolCues** ($1/student/month, ~$75/mo min) is the price-value incumbent — broad modules, affordable, 10+ years in the market. But its billing is confusing and hard to navigate, the UI is clunky, there is no CRM or admissions pipeline, no teacher observation notes, no health profiles, no contract versioning, and no budget tracking. Customers love the support team but frequently cite the software itself as frustrating.

**Spark School Software** ($34.99/mo) was built "by microschoolers for microschoolers" and has the right identity — but very little depth. No CRM, no health forms, no contracts, no application pipeline, no tour booking, no budget module, no behavioral notes. Works for schools that need just a basic gradebook and payment link.

**Brightwheel** ($600M+ valuation) is the category giant in childcare/daycare — exceptional parent UX, strong brand, viral word-of-mouth. But it is a childcare product, not a school product. No academic SIS, no enrollment wizard, no learning profiles, no CRM, no contracts. The lesson to take from Brightwheel: exceptional parent UX drives viral adoption. That is a design principle built into School Stack from day one.

**Omella** (free + $1.99/transaction) handles payments and forms for microschools at zero monthly cost — strong hook for new founders. But it is not a school management system. No parent portal, no teacher dashboard, no CRM, no applications pipeline. Founders who start with Omella outgrow it quickly. That is a direct acquisition opportunity for School Stack.

**PowerSchool, MySchoolWorx, Kangarootime** are all built for the wrong audience — large districts, faith-based K-12, or childcare centers. Wrong scale, wrong assumptions, wrong price.

### The gap nobody fills

Every competitor fails at the same things: a unified CRM from first inquiry to enrolled student, a multi-step application wizard with health forms and waivers, contract versioning with digital signatures, student health and behavioral profiles, teacher observation notes with attachments, budget tracking, multi-type Stripe checkout, and real-time operational notifications.

**School Stack already has all of these — in production, proven at a real school.**

### Our positioning

School Stack occupies the position no competitor holds: high microschool specificity combined with enterprise-grade operational depth. The question we ask every prospect is not "does your current software have a gradebook?" — it is "does your current software handle everything from the first inquiry to the signed contract to the first tuition payment in one unified system, with zero data re-entry?"

---

## Why It's Different

**Built by a founder, for founders.** School Stack was not designed by a software company guessing at school needs. It was built by someone who ran Sage Field from 0 to 30 students using it. Every feature exists because a real microschool needed it. That is a differentiation that money cannot buy and cannot be copied.

**The full customer journey in one system.** The public marketing site, tour booking, waitlist, application, contract, tuition, daily feed, and messaging are all one product. No other platform in this price range manages the experience from "stranger on the internet sees your school" through "enrolled student in the classroom" without leaving the system.

**Operational depth at small school pricing.** Enterprise features — contract versioning with diff viewer, multi-type Stripe checkout with webhook automation, student health profiles, behavioral strategies, CRM with tags and call logs, budget module with bank sync — at $79–$249/month.

**Concierge setup.** Every customer gets their instance configured with their school's name, domain, programs, and branding. This is not a DIY SaaS product where they figure it out themselves. It is a ready-to-use system, handed over.

**Not a childcare product wearing a school costume.** Every assumption in the platform is built around academic microschools: multi-step enrollment, parent-partnership models, flexible programs, small teams, close relationships, and founders doing everything themselves.

---

## Business Model

Customers pay a monthly or annual subscription for their Stack tier. Annual plans include two months free, which improves lifetime value and reduces seasonal churn.

On top of the subscription, schools commission Custom Stack features — bespoke additions scoped, priced, and built specifically for their school. This is the highest-margin tier and the strongest competitive moat: a school that has custom workflows built for them is deeply embedded and unlikely to switch.

The relationship is ongoing. As a school grows, they upgrade their Stack. As their needs get more specific, they commission more custom work. Concierge onboarding is included for the first 10 customers and available as a premium add-on beyond that.

## Go-to-Market Strategy

### Phase 1 — Foundation (Months 1–3)

- Finalize the Sage Field case study with specific metrics: enrollment applications processed, revenue collected, hours saved per week, parent satisfaction
- Launch the School Stack website with the interactive product demo (tabbed preview, founder video, feature comparison vs. SchoolCues and Spark)
- Form School Stack LLC (separate from Sage Field LLC)
- Apply to the National Microschooling Center preferred provider directory — Spark is already listed; this is table stakes
- Close the first 3 paying customers via personal demo before any paid advertising

### Phase 2 — Community-Led Acquisition (Months 2–6)

Microschool founders concentrate in specific, highly accessible communities. Zero-cost acquisition:

- Facebook groups: Microschool Revolution, "Microschool Founders," state-specific homeschool groups (Texas alone has tens of thousands of members), NMC member community
- Approach: add value for weeks before ever mentioning the product. Answer operational questions. Share free resources. When someone posts about software pain, respond with a genuine answer and offer to show how you solved it at Sage Field.
- Lead magnets: free downloadable templates (enrollment agreement, liability waiver, parent handbook, health form) — all already built for Sage Field, branded School Stack, gated behind an email

### Phase 3 — Content & SEO (Months 3–9)

High-priority content targeting low-competition, high-intent keywords:

- "How to start a microschool in Texas"
- "Best microschool software 2025–2026"
- "SchoolCues alternatives"
- "How to manage microschool enrollment"
- "Microschool parent communication software"
- "How to collect tuition for a microschool"
- "Microschool application form template"

### Phase 4 — Partnerships (Months 4–12)

- **KaiPod Catalyst** — trains new microschool founders; be the recommended software at graduation
- **Microschool Revolution** — partner with their founder directory
- **National Microschooling Center** — preferred provider status + member pricing
- **Texas homeschool associations (THSC)** — sponsor events, exhibit at conferences, offer member pricing
- **Outschool, Prenda, and other microschool networks** — single partnership converts at scale

### Phase 5 — Direct Outreach (Ongoing)

The highest-converting channel for school software is direct, personal outreach from the founder. The story — "I built this for my own school, it worked, want to see it?" — closes faster than any ad.

Outreach sequence:

1. Find founders via Facebook groups, Instagram, NMC directory, Microschool Revolution listings
2. Engage with their content authentically for 1–2 weeks
3. DM or email: "I built the software for my own microschool, Sage Field — grew from 0 to 30 students with it. Happy to show you what it does in 15 minutes. No pitch, just a demo."
4. Demo → free trial → close

Microschool founders are solo operators making fast decisions. Unlike K-12 districts (6–12 month sales cycles), a well-positioned microschool deal can close in days.

---

## 90-Day Launch Checklist

- [ ] Form School Stack LLC in Texas (separate from Sage Field LLC)
- [ ] Secure schoolstack.com or equivalent domain
- [ ] Document Sage Field case study with specific metrics
- [ ] Build School Stack marketing website with interactive product demo and case study page
- [ ] Apply to NMC preferred provider directory
- [ ] Join 10+ microschool/homeschool Facebook groups; begin value-first engagement
- [ ] Publish first 3 SEO-targeted blog posts
- [ ] Create lead magnet package (enrollment agreement template + parent handbook + health form)
- [ ] Set up analytics (PostHog or equivalent) on the School Stack site
- [ ] Finalize pricing tiers and build Stripe billing for School Stack subscriptions
- [ ] Finalize annual plan pricing (2 months free) and 14-day free trial setup
- [ ] Reach out to 20 microschool founders with personal demo offer
- [ ] Close first 3 paying customers before any paid advertising
- [ ] Attend or present at one Texas homeschool/alternative education event
- [ ] Reach out to KaiPod Catalyst about partnership

---

## Proven at Sage Field

School Stack is not a pitch deck. It is running right now at Sage Field Microschool in Texas — managing real enrollment applications, real families, real payments, real teacher assignments, and real school operations. The platform has been through the full enrollment cycle, handled Stripe payments, managed waitlists, sent emails, tracked leads, and powered a parent and teacher portal used by real people.

That's the foundation every new customer is buying — not a prototype, not a beta, but a working system that was pressure-tested before it was ever sold.

---

## The Thesis

The microschool software market has a clear gap: there is no platform that provides operational completeness — from marketing and lead capture through enrollment, contracts, billing, parent communication, and admin operations — at a price and with a UX that small school founders can actually adopt.

SchoolCues is cheap but shallow. Spark is microschool-native but limited. Brightwheel is excellent but built for childcare. Omella handles payments but nothing else.

School Stack has already built what does not yet exist in this market. The go-to-market strategy is not about building — it is about distribution, community, and story. The founder-led story, the Sage Field proof case, the depth of the platform, and the specificity of the target market create a competitive position that well-funded competitors cannot easily replicate.

The goal is to be the first name every microschool founder hears when they ask "what software should I use to run my school?" — and the last one they ever need to switch away from.

input.prompt:
Design a detailed implementation plan for a School Stack landing page. This is a high-fidelity marketing page — the user wants a masterclass in UI/UX, no vibe-coded look, professional, built to convert school business owners.

## Project Context

- **Stack**: Next.js 16.2.4, React 19.2.4, TypeScript, Tailwind CSS 4.2.2
- **Location**: /Users/juliuscecilia/Desktop/schoolstack/schoolstack_landing/
- **Current state**: Blank slate — only src/app/page.tsx (empty), src/app/globals.css (@import "tailwindcss"), src/app/layout.tsx (bare bones)
- **Framer-motion**: NOT installed yet. Must be installed.
- **Next.js 16 key change**: All request APIs are async (params, cookies, headers). For a static marketing page this doesn't matter much since we have no dynamic routes.
- **Tailwind v4**: No tailwind.config.js. Configuration done via `@theme` in globals.css. Utility classes map from theme tokens:
  - `--color-{name}` → `bg-{name}`, `text-{name}`, `border-{name}`
  - `--radius-{name}` → `rounded-{name}`
  - `--shadow-{name}` → `shadow-{name}`
  - `--font-{name}` → `font-{name}`

## Design Spec Summary

**Design tokens (colors)**:
```css
--bg: #f6f5f2; --bg-alt: #f1efe9; --surface: #ffffff; --surface-soft: #fbfaf7; --surface-muted: #f3f1ec;
--border: #e7e3dc; --border-strong: #d9d4cc;
--text: #1f1e1a; --text-muted: #6f6b65; --text-faint: #9a958d;
--accent: #244b46; --accent-hover: #1d3d39; --accent-soft: #dfe8e5;
```

**Fonts**: Instrument Serif (display) + Inter (body/UI) via next/font/google

**Page sections** (in order):
1. Navbar — transparent → surface on scroll past 40px, logo left + nav links center + CTA right, mobile drawer
2. HeroSection — founder-led editorial split, text left (45%), product visual right (55%), eyebrow "Built inside a microschool", H1: "We built the software we couldn't find to run our own school."
3. PainSection — centered narrow column, H2 "Most microschools are too big for spreadsheets and too small for enterprise software.", pain tool strip (Google Forms, Venmo, Gmail, etc.)
4. FounderStoryBridgeSection — 2-col asymmetric, left story text, right collage of product crop + founder note card + school facts card
5. ProductPreviewSection — 7-tab interactive product preview (Website, Enrollment, Admin [default], Parents, Teachers, Payments, Leads) with placeholder HTML/CSS mockups for each tab
6. WorkflowSection — eyebrow "How it works", H2 "From founder chaos to a school that runs cleanly in three steps.", 3 step cards with connecting line
7. StacksSection — 3 tier cards (Starter $79, Growth $149 [featured], Academy $249), no price shown on page
8. ProofSection — 2-col, left text + pull quote, right casual stacked screenshot collage
9. CustomSection — full-width surface-muted band, centered, tag cloud of custom options
10. FinalCTASection — --accent full-bleed green background, "Your school deserves software built by someone who understands the work."
11. Footer — 4 cols

**Motion**: framer-motion whileInView (once: true), entrance y: 16-20 → 0 + fade, stagger groups

## Required Output

Provide a detailed implementation plan including:

1. **File structure** — exact paths for all new files
2. **Package installs** needed (framer-motion version?)
3. **globals.css** — complete @theme block with all design tokens mapped to Tailwind utility names
4. **layout.tsx** — font setup approach with next/font/google
5. **Component architecture** — which components are 'use client' vs server, why
6. **Each section** — key Tailwind classes, layout approach, tricky implementation details
7. **ProductPreviewSection mockups** — specific HTML/CSS structure for each of the 7 tabs (kanban, form, checklist, etc.)
8. **Placeholder product visual** — for hero section and other product frames
9. **Animations** — specific framer-motion patterns to use per section
10. **Mobile responsive** — key breakpoint behaviors
11. **Font variable naming** — how Tailwind v4 `@theme` font variables connect to next/font CSS variables

Be very specific about Tailwind class names, especially for Tailwind v4 where custom tokens become utilities. Flag any Tailwind v4 specifics that differ from v3.

content.text:
Now I have everything I need. Let me build the comprehensive implementation plan.

---

# School Stack Landing Page — Complete Implementation Plan

---

## 1. Package Installs

```bash
npm install framer-motion@12.38.0
```

framer-motion v12 is the current stable release with full React 19 / Next.js 16 compatibility. It ships ESM-first, which pairs cleanly with Next.js's bundler module resolution (`"moduleResolution": "bundler"` in your tsconfig). No additional type packages needed — types are bundled.

**Important Tailwind v4 note**: Tailwind v4 uses `@tailwindcss/postcss` as its PostCSS plugin, which is already in your devDependencies. No `tailwind.config.js` is created. All custom tokens live inside an `@theme` block in `globals.css`.

---

## 2. File Structure

```
src/
├── app/
│   ├── globals.css                          ← complete @theme block + base styles
│   ├── layout.tsx                           ← font setup, metadata, body classes
│   └── page.tsx                             ← composes all sections in order
│
├── components/
│   ├── ui/                                  ← atomic primitives
│   │   ├── Button.tsx                       ← primary / secondary / ghost variants
│   │   ├── Badge.tsx                        ← eyebrow chips
│   │   ├── Container.tsx                    ← max-width wrapper
│   │   └── FadeInView.tsx                   ← reusable framer-motion entrance wrapper
│   │
│   └── sections/                            ← one file per section
│       ├── Navbar.tsx                       ← 'use client' (scroll state, drawer state)
│       ├── HeroSection.tsx                  ← 'use client' (entrance animation)
│       ├── PainSection.tsx                  ← server (static content, CSS-only scroll reveal via FadeInView inside)
│       ├── FounderStoryBridgeSection.tsx    ← server
│       ├── ProductPreviewSection.tsx        ← 'use client' (tab state)
│       ├── WorkflowSection.tsx              ← server
│       ├── StacksSection.tsx                ← server
│       ├── ProofSection.tsx                 ← server
│       ├── CustomSection.tsx                ← server
│       ├── FinalCTASection.tsx              ← server
│       └── Footer.tsx                       ← server
│
└── lib/
    └── motion.ts                            ← shared framer-motion variants
```

**Why this structure**: The design spec references `components/marketing/` as the preferred folder. Using `components/sections/` is equivalent and cleaner for a single-page build. `components/ui/` holds the reusable primitives. `lib/motion.ts` centralises animation variants so they are consistent across all sections.

---

## 3. globals.css — Complete @theme Block

Tailwind v4 maps `@theme` tokens directly to utility classes. The naming rule is critical: `--color-{name}` becomes `bg-{name}`, `text-{name}`, `border-{name}`. The dashes in names like `surface-soft` map to `bg-surface-soft`. Custom properties that are NOT under `--color-*` do not auto-generate utilities — they become CSS variables only (accessible via `var(--name)`).

```css
@import "tailwindcss";

@theme {
  /* ─── Colors ─────────────────────────────────────────── */
  --color-bg:            #f6f5f2;
  --color-bg-alt:        #f1efe9;
  --color-surface:       #ffffff;
  --color-surface-soft:  #fbfaf7;
  --color-surface-muted: #f3f1ec;
  --color-border:        #e7e3dc;
  --color-border-strong: #d9d4cc;
  --color-text:          #1f1e1a;
  --color-text-muted:    #6f6b65;
  --color-text-faint:    #9a958d;
  --color-accent:        #244b46;
  --color-accent-hover:  #1d3d39;
  --color-accent-soft:   #dfe8e5;

  /* ─── Radius ─────────────────────────────────────────── */
  --radius-sm:   10px;
  --radius-md:   14px;
  --radius-lg:   20px;
  --radius-xl:   28px;
  --radius-pill: 9999px;

  /* ─── Shadows ─────────────────────────────────────────── */
  --shadow-xs: 0 1px 2px rgba(20,20,20,0.04);
  --shadow-sm: 0 6px 24px rgba(20,20,20,0.05);
  --shadow-md: 0 14px 40px rgba(20,20,20,0.08);
  --shadow-lg: 0 24px 60px rgba(20,20,20,0.10);

  /* ─── Typography ─────────────────────────────────────── */
  /* These connect to next/font CSS variables — see layout.tsx */
  --font-display: var(--font-instrument-serif), "Instrument Serif", serif;
  --font-body:    var(--font-inter), "Inter", sans-serif;

  /* ─── Easing (CSS vars only, not utilities) ─────────── */
  --ease-standard: cubic-bezier(0.16, 1, 0.3, 1);
  --dur-fast:  180ms;
  --dur-med:   280ms;
  --dur-slow:  480ms;
}

/* ─── Base ──────────────────────────────────────────────── */
html {
  scroll-behavior: smooth;
}

body {
  background-color: var(--color-bg);
  color: var(--color-text);
  font-family: var(--font-body);
  -webkit-font-smoothing: antialiased;
  -moz-osx-font-smoothing: grayscale;
  text-rendering: optimizeLegibility;
}

h1, h2, h3, .font-display {
  font-family: var(--font-display);
  letter-spacing: -0.03em;
  line-height: 0.98;
}

@media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) {
  *, *::before, *::after {
    animation-duration: 0.01ms !important;
    transition-duration: 0.01ms !important;
  }
}
```

**Tailwind v4 critical notes**:
- In v4, `--color-*` tokens auto-generate the full color utility family (`bg-*`, `text-*`, `border-*`, `ring-*`, `fill-*`, etc.). You do NOT need to write `bg-[var(--color-accent)]` — just write `bg-accent`.
- `--radius-*` → `rounded-sm`, `rounded-md`, `rounded-lg`, `rounded-xl`, `rounded-pill`.
- `--shadow-*` → `shadow-xs`, `shadow-sm`, `shadow-md`, `shadow-lg`.
- `--font-display` and `--font-body` → `font-display`, `font-body` utilities (applied as `font-family`).
- There is NO `--color-` prefix needed in class names. `bg-surface-soft` just works.

---

## 4. layout.tsx — Font Setup

`Instrument Serif` is NOT a variable font — it has fixed weights (400, 400 italic). `Inter` IS a variable font. The correct next/font approach:

```tsx
import { Inter, Instrument_Serif } from 'next/font/google'

const inter = Inter({
  subsets: ['latin'],
  display: 'swap',
  variable: '--font-inter',
})

const instrumentSerif = Instrument_Serif({
  subsets: ['latin'],
  weight: ['400'],
  style: ['normal', 'italic'],
  display: 'swap',
  variable: '--font-instrument-serif',
})
```

In the layout body:
```tsx
<html lang="en" className={`${inter.variable} ${instrumentSerif.variable}`}>
  <body className="font-body antialiased">
    {children}
  </body>
</html>
```

**How the font chain works**: `next/font` injects `--font-inter` and `--font-instrument-serif` as CSS custom properties on the `html` element. In `globals.css`, the `@theme` block defines `--font-display: var(--font-instrument-serif), ...` which Tailwind v4 picks up and exposes as `font-display`. When you write `font-display` on an element, it resolves through the chain: Tailwind utility → `--font-display` CSS var → `--font-instrument-serif` CSS var → the actual font stack. This is the correct v4 pattern — no `tailwind.config.js` override needed.

**Instrument Serif note**: This font is `Instrument_Serif` (underscore) in `next/font/google` imports, not `Instrument Serif`.

## 5. lib/motion.ts — Shared Animation Variants

```typescript
import type { Variants } from 'framer-motion'

export const fadeUp: Variants = {
  hidden: { opacity: 0, y: 18 },
  visible: {
    opacity: 1,
    y: 0,
    transition: { duration: 0.55, ease: [0.16, 1, 0.3, 1] },
  },
}

export const fadeIn: Variants = {
  hidden: { opacity: 0 },
  visible: {
    opacity: 1,
    transition: { duration: 0.45, ease: [0.16, 1, 0.3, 1] },
  },
}

export const staggerContainer: Variants = {
  hidden: {},
  visible: {
    transition: { staggerChildren: 0.08, delayChildren: 0.05 },
  },
}

export const staggerContainerSlow: Variants = {
  hidden: {},
  visible: {
    transition: { staggerChildren: 0.12, delayChildren: 0.1 },
  },
}

export const scaleIn: Variants = {
  hidden: { opacity: 0, scale: 0.97 },
  visible: {
    opacity: 1,
    scale: 1,
    transition: { duration: 0.6, ease: [0.16, 1, 0.3, 1] },
  },
}

// Standard whileInView props — use spread: {...inViewProps}
export const inViewProps = {
  initial: 'hidden',
  whileInView: 'visible',
  viewport: { once: true, amount: 0.2 },
} as const
```

## 6. FadeInView.tsx — Reusable Entrance Wrapper

```tsx
'use client'
import { motion } from 'framer-motion'
import { fadeUp, inViewProps } from '@/lib/motion'

export function FadeInView({
  children,
  delay = 0,
  className,
}: {
  children: React.ReactNode
  delay?: number
  className?: string
}) {
  return (
    <motion.div
      {...inViewProps}
      variants={fadeUp}
      transition={{ delay }}
      className={className}
    >
      {children}
    </motion.div>
  )
}
```

## 7. Component Architecture — Server vs Client

| Component | Directive | Reason |
|---|---|---|
| `Navbar` | `'use client'` | `useState` for scroll opacity and mobile drawer open/close |
| `HeroSection` | `'use client'` | `motion.div` with `useReducedMotion()` check, entrance animation on mount |
| `FadeInView` | `'use client'` | `motion.div` is a client component |
| `ProductPreviewSection` | `'use client'` | `useState` for active tab, `AnimatePresence` for cross-fade |
| `PainSection` | Server | Static HTML; uses `FadeInView` (client) as a child, which is fine |
| `FounderStoryBridgeSection` | Server | Static HTML; uses `FadeInView` |
| `WorkflowSection` | Server | Static content |
| `StacksSection` | Server | Static content |
| `ProofSection` | Server | Static content |
| `CustomSection` | Server | Static content |
| `FinalCTASection` | Server | Static content |
| `Footer` | Server | Static content |
| `Button` | Server | No interactivity beyond CSS hover |
| `Badge` | Server | Pure display |
| `Container` | Server | Layout wrapper only |

Server components are the default in Next.js App Router. Only components that require browser APIs, event handlers, or React hooks need `'use client'`. The key pattern: a server component CAN render a client component child — the client subtree takes over. So `PainSection` is a server component that renders `<FadeInView>` (client) children, which is the optimal pattern for reducing client-side JS.

Tags (12 total from spec): Custom enrollment flows · Multi-location support · SMS integrations · Attendance tracking · Custom reporting · Google Calendar sync · Custom tuition structures · White-label branding · CRM integrations · Volunteer management · Learning plan tools · Any workflow you need

CTA: `mt-10 inline-flex items-center gap-2 bg-accent text-white rounded-pill px-6 h-11 text-sm font-medium hover:bg-accent-hover hover:-translate-y-0.5 transition-all`

**Animation**: Tags stagger in individually with `staggerContainer` — 12 children × 40ms = ~480ms total reveal.

### Section 10 — FinalCTASection

**File**: `src/components/sections/FinalCTASection.tsx`

**Layout**: `bg-accent py-32`. `max-w-[720px] mx-auto px-6 text-center`.

H2: `font-display text-[clamp(2.2rem,4.5vw,3.5rem)] leading-[1.02] text-[#f8f7f4]`

Body: `text-[17px] text-[rgba(248,247,244,0.7)] mt-6 max-w-[52ch] mx-auto`

CTA button: `mt-10 bg-[#f8f7f4] text-accent rounded-pill px-8 h-12 text-sm font-medium hover:bg-white hover:-translate-y-0.5 transition-all`

Reassurance: `text-sm text-[rgba(248,247,244,0.5)] mt-5`

**Note on Tailwind v4 arbitrary colors**: For the light-on-dark text colors (`rgba(248,247,244,0.7)`), use `text-[rgba(248,247,244,0.7)]` — this works the same as v3 arbitrary values.

### Section 11 — Footer

**File**: `src/components/sections/Footer.tsx`

**Layout**: `bg-bg border-t border-border py-16`. `max-w-[1200px] mx-auto px-6 lg:px-16`.

Top grid: `grid grid-cols-2 md:grid-cols-4 gap-10 lg:gap-16`.

Column 1 (brand): School Stack wordmark in `font-display text-lg`, one-liner `text-sm text-text-muted mt-2`.

Columns 2-4 (links): `text-xs font-medium uppercase tracking-widest text-text-faint mb-4` for headers. Links: `text-sm text-text-muted hover:text-text transition-colors block py-0.5`.

Bottom bar: `border-t border-border mt-12 pt-6 flex flex-col md:flex-row justify-between items-center gap-3`. Copyright: `text-xs text-text-faint`. "Built by the team at Sage Field" in accent.

---

## 9. ui/Button.tsx

```tsx
import { cn } from '@/lib/utils' // or inline

type ButtonVariant = 'primary' | 'secondary' | 'ghost'

interface ButtonProps extends React.ButtonHTMLAttributes<HTMLButtonElement> {
  variant?: ButtonVariant
  size?: 'sm' | 'md' | 'lg'
  as?: 'button' | 'a'
  href?: string
}
```

Classes per variant:
- primary: `bg-accent text-white hover:bg-accent-hover hover:-translate-y-0.5 shadow-xs`
- secondary: `bg-surface border border-border text-text hover:bg-surface-muted hover:-translate-y-0.5`
- ghost: `text-text-muted hover:text-text hover:bg-surface-muted`

Shared: `inline-flex items-center justify-center gap-2 rounded-pill font-medium transition-all duration-200 cursor-pointer`

Sizes: sm `h-9 px-4 text-[13px]`, md `h-11 px-5 text-sm`, lg `h-12 px-7 text-[15px]`

---

## 10. Mobile Responsive Key Behaviors

### Breakpoint system
- `sm`: 640px (not used heavily — skip straight to `md`)
- `md`: 768px — most grids switch from 1-col to multi-col
- `lg`: 1024px — hero splits, asymmetric layouts activate
- `xl`: 1280px — max container widths

### Hero: `< lg`
- Single column, visual below text
- H1 reduces via `clamp()` naturally
- CTA row stacks to `flex-col` at `< sm` if buttons are long
- Product frame: `aspect-[4/3]` not fixed height

### Navbar: `< md`
- Hide center nav links (`hidden md:flex`)
- Show hamburger `md:hidden`
- Drawer full-screen from right

### ProductPreview: All sizes
- Tab bar: `overflow-x-auto` with `flex-nowrap`
- Frame: `h-[280px]` mobile vs `h-[560px]` desktop
- Caption bar always visible (not hidden on mobile)
- Active tab scrolled into view via `ref.scrollIntoView({ behavior: 'smooth', inline: 'center' })`

### PainSection pain strip: `< md`
- `flex-nowrap overflow-x-auto` instead of `flex-wrap`
- Horizontal scrollable row with hidden scrollbar

### FounderStoryBridge collage: `< lg`
- Collage rendered as a simple grid of 2 cards instead of the layered absolute positioning
- `grid grid-cols-2 gap-3` for the two smaller cards
- Product frame above at full width

### StacksSection: `< md`
- Single column stack, featured card loses the `-mt-4` elevation (flat stack)
- Featured badge still shows

### ProofSection collage: `< lg`
- Stacked cards converted to a simple list of 2 cards in a column (no overlap/rotation)

### FinalCTA: All sizes
- H2 already uses `clamp()` so responsive by nature
- Padding: `py-24 md:py-32`

---

## 11. page.tsx Composition

```tsx
import Navbar from '@/components/sections/Navbar'
import HeroSection from '@/components/sections/HeroSection'
import PainSection from '@/components/sections/PainSection'
import FounderStoryBridgeSection from '@/components/sections/FounderStoryBridgeSection'
import ProductPreviewSection from '@/components/sections/ProductPreviewSection'
import WorkflowSection from '@/components/sections/WorkflowSection'
import StacksSection from '@/components/sections/StacksSection'
import ProofSection from '@/components/sections/ProofSection'
import CustomSection from '@/components/sections/CustomSection'
import FinalCTASection from '@/components/sections/FinalCTASection'
import Footer from '@/components/sections/Footer'

export default function Home() {
  return (
    <>
      <Navbar />
      <main>
        <HeroSection />
        <PainSection />
        <FounderStoryBridgeSection />
        <ProductPreviewSection />
        <WorkflowSection />
        <StacksSection />
        <ProofSection />
        <CustomSection />
        <FinalCTASection />
      </main>
      <Footer />
    </>
  )
}
```

`page.tsx` stays a server component. All client interactivity is isolated in the child components that declare `'use client'`.

---

## 12. Motion Choreography Summary

| Section | Pattern | Trigger |
|---|---|---|
| Navbar | CSS `transition-all` on scroll | JS scroll listener |
| Hero | `initial/animate` stagger (not whileInView) | On mount |
| Hero product frame | `scaleIn` variant, 320ms delay | On mount |
| PainSection | `whileInView staggerContainer` → H2, body, strip | Scroll enter |
| FounderStoryBridge | Left: `fadeUp whileInView`. Right: `fadeUp` 120ms delay | Scroll enter |
| WorkflowSection | `staggerContainer` over 3 cards, 120ms stagger | Scroll enter |
| StacksSection | `staggerContainer` over 3 cards, 100ms stagger | Scroll enter |
| ProofSection | Left: `fadeUp`. Right collage: `fadeUp` delayed | Scroll enter |
| CustomSection | H2+body first, then tags `staggerContainer` 40ms | Scroll enter |
| FinalCTASection | H2+body `fadeUp`, then CTA 80ms later | Scroll enter |
| ProductPreview tab switch | `AnimatePresence mode="wait"` opacity 0→1, 220ms | Tab click |
| Mobile drawer | `framer-motion x: '100%' → 0`, `ease-standard` | Hamburger click |

**All `whileInView`** use `viewport={{ once: true, amount: 0.2 }}` to prevent re-animation on scroll-back.

**`prefers-reduced-motion`**: The CSS block in globals.css handles this at the CSS level. For framer-motion, use `useReducedMotion()` in the two client components that run on mount (Navbar, HeroSection) and short-circuit the animation values when true.

---

## 13. Tailwind v4 Specifics vs v3 — Critical Differences

1. **No `tailwind.config.js`**: All token definitions live in `@theme {}` inside globals.css. No `extend.colors`, no `extend.borderRadius`, etc.

2. **`--color-*` naming is mandatory**: To get `bg-accent`, you MUST define `--color-accent`. Defining `--accent` alone does NOT create a Tailwind utility — it becomes a CSS variable only. This is the most common mistake when migrating.

3. **`@theme` vs `:root`**: Design tokens used ONLY as CSS variables (not needing Tailwind utilities) can stay in `:root`. Tokens that need to generate utilities MUST be in `@theme`. The shadows, easing values, and duration values should stay in `:root` because Tailwind v4 has its own shadow scale — don't fight it. Use `shadow-[var(--shadow-md)]` as an escape hatch, or redefine `--shadow-*` in `@theme` to override Tailwind's defaults.

4. **`--radius-pill: 9999px`** maps to `rounded-pill`. In v3 you'd write `rounded-full`. In v4, since you're defining your own radius token, `rounded-pill` is the correct class.

5. **`--font-display` and `--font-body`**: In `@theme`, these create `font-display` and `font-body` utility classes that apply `font-family`. This is correct v4 behavior — no need for `fontFamily` in a config object.

6. **Arbitrary values still work**: `text-[clamp(2rem,5vw,5.5rem)]`, `bg-[rgba(248,247,244,0.7)]`, `h-[540px]` all work identically to v3.

7. **No `bg-opacity-*` utilities**: Tailwind v4 uses `bg-accent/50` syntax (slash opacity) natively. Same as v3's modern syntax.

8. **`border-border`**: Since you defined `--color-border`, you get `border-border` automatically. You do NOT need `border-[var(--color-border)]`.

---

## 14. Implementation Sequence

1. Run `npm install framer-motion@12.38.0`
2. Write `src/app/globals.css` with complete `@theme` block
3. Write `src/app/layout.tsx` with font imports and CSS variables applied to `html`
4. Create `src/lib/motion.ts` with shared variants
5. Create `src/components/ui/` primitives: `FadeInView.tsx`, `Button.tsx`, `Badge.tsx`, `Container.tsx`
6. Build sections in page order — `Navbar` first (always visible), then top-to-bottom
7. Wire everything in `src/app/page.tsx`
8. Test mobile at 390px (iPhone), 768px (tablet), 1440px (wide desktop)

---

### Critical Files for Implementation

- `/Users/juliuscecilia/Desktop/schoolstack/schoolstack_landing/src/app/globals.css`
- `/Users/juliuscecilia/Desktop/schoolstack/schoolstack_landing/src/app/layout.tsx`
- `/Users/juliuscecilia/Desktop/schoolstack/schoolstack_landing/src/lib/motion.ts`
- `/Users/juliuscecilia/Desktop/schoolstack/schoolstack_landing/src/components/sections/ProductPreviewSection.tsx`
- `/Users/juliuscecilia/Desktop/schoolstack/schoolstack_landing/src/components/sections/Navbar.tsx`

Now I have everything I need. Let me build the comprehensive implementation plan.

---

# School Stack Landing Page — Complete Implementation Plan

---

## 1. Package Installs

```bash
npm install framer-motion@12.38.0
```

framer-motion v12 is the current stable release with full React 19 / Next.js 16 compatibility. It ships ESM-first, which pairs cleanly with Next.js's bundler module resolution (`"moduleResolution": "bundler"` in your tsconfig). No additional type packages needed — types are bundled.

**Important Tailwind v4 note**: Tailwind v4 uses `@tailwindcss/postcss` as its PostCSS plugin, which is already in your devDependencies. No `tailwind.config.js` is created. All custom tokens live inside an `@theme` block in `globals.css`.

---

## 2. File Structure

```
src/
├── app/
│   ├── globals.css                          ← complete @theme block + base styles
│   ├── layout.tsx                           ← font setup, metadata, body classes
│   └── page.tsx                             ← composes all sections in order
│
├── components/
│   ├── ui/                                  ← atomic primitives
│   │   ├── Button.tsx                       ← primary / secondary / ghost variants
│   │   ├── Badge.tsx                        ← eyebrow chips
│   │   ├── Container.tsx                    ← max-width wrapper
│   │   └── FadeInView.tsx                   ← reusable framer-motion entrance wrapper
│   │
│   └── sections/                            ← one file per section
│       ├── Navbar.tsx                       ← 'use client' (scroll state, drawer state)
│       ├── HeroSection.tsx                  ← 'use client' (entrance animation)
│       ├── PainSection.tsx                  ← server (static content, CSS-only scroll reveal via FadeInView inside)
│       ├── FounderStoryBridgeSection.tsx    ← server
│       ├── ProductPreviewSection.tsx        ← 'use client' (tab state)
│       ├── WorkflowSection.tsx              ← server
│       ├── StacksSection.tsx                ← server
│       ├── ProofSection.tsx                 ← server
│       ├── CustomSection.tsx                ← server
│       ├── FinalCTASection.tsx              ← server
│       └── Footer.tsx                       ← server
│
└── lib/
    └── motion.ts                            ← shared framer-motion variants
```

**Why this structure**: The design spec references `components/marketing/` as the preferred folder. Using `components/sections/` is equivalent and cleaner for a single-page build. `components/ui/` holds the reusable primitives. `lib/motion.ts` centralises animation variants so they are consistent across all sections.

---

## 3. globals.css — Complete @theme Block

Tailwind v4 maps `@theme` tokens directly to utility classes. The naming rule is critical: `--color-{name}` becomes `bg-{name}`, `text-{name}`, `border-{name}`. The dashes in names like `surface-soft` map to `bg-surface-soft`. Custom properties that are NOT under `--color-*` do not auto-generate utilities — they become CSS variables only (accessible via `var(--name)`).

```css
@import "tailwindcss";

@theme {
  /* ─── Colors ─────────────────────────────────────────── */
  --color-bg:            #f6f5f2;
  --color-bg-alt:        #f1efe9;
  --color-surface:       #ffffff;
  --color-surface-soft:  #fbfaf7;
  --color-surface-muted: #f3f1ec;
  --color-border:        #e7e3dc;
  --color-border-strong: #d9d4cc;
  --color-text:          #1f1e1a;
  --color-text-muted:    #6f6b65;
  --color-text-faint:    #9a958d;
  --color-accent:        #244b46;
  --color-accent-hover:  #1d3d39;
  --color-accent-soft:   #dfe8e5;

  /* ─── Radius ─────────────────────────────────────────── */
  --radius-sm:   10px;
  --radius-md:   14px;
  --radius-lg:   20px;
  --radius-xl:   28px;
  --radius-pill: 9999px;

  /* ─── Shadows ─────────────────────────────────────────── */
  --shadow-xs: 0 1px 2px rgba(20,20,20,0.04);
  --shadow-sm: 0 6px 24px rgba(20,20,20,0.05);
  --shadow-md: 0 14px 40px rgba(20,20,20,0.08);
  --shadow-lg: 0 24px 60px rgba(20,20,20,0.10);

  /* ─── Typography ─────────────────────────────────────── */
  /* These connect to next/font CSS variables — see layout.tsx */
  --font-display: var(--font-instrument-serif), "Instrument Serif", serif;
  --font-body:    var(--font-inter), "Inter", sans-serif;

  /* ─── Easing (CSS vars only, not utilities) ─────────── */
  --ease-standard: cubic-bezier(0.16, 1, 0.3, 1);
  --dur-fast:  180ms;
  --dur-med:   280ms;
  --dur-slow:  480ms;
}

/* ─── Base ──────────────────────────────────────────────── */
html {
  scroll-behavior: smooth;
}

body {
  background-color: var(--color-bg);
  color: var(--color-text);
  font-family: var(--font-body);
  -webkit-font-smoothing: antialiased;
  -moz-osx-font-smoothing: grayscale;
  text-rendering: optimizeLegibility;
}

h1, h2, h3, .font-display {
  font-family: var(--font-display);
  letter-spacing: -0.03em;
  line-height: 0.98;
}

@media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) {
  *, *::before, *::after {
    animation-duration: 0.01ms !important;
    transition-duration: 0.01ms !important;
  }
}
```

**Tailwind v4 critical notes**:
- In v4, `--color-*` tokens auto-generate the full color utility family (`bg-*`, `text-*`, `border-*`, `ring-*`, `fill-*`, etc.). You do NOT need to write `bg-[var(--color-accent)]` — just write `bg-accent`.
- `--radius-*` → `rounded-sm`, `rounded-md`, `rounded-lg`, `rounded-xl`, `rounded-pill`.
- `--shadow-*` → `shadow-xs`, `shadow-sm`, `shadow-md`, `shadow-lg`.
- `--font-display` and `--font-body` → `font-display`, `font-body` utilities (applied as `font-family`).
- There is NO `--color-` prefix needed in class names. `bg-surface-soft` just works.

---

## 4. layout.tsx — Font Setup

`Instrument Serif` is NOT a variable font — it has fixed weights (400, 400 italic). `Inter` IS a variable font. The correct next/font approach:

```tsx
import { Inter, Instrument_Serif } from 'next/font/google'

const inter = Inter({
  subsets: ['latin'],
  display: 'swap',
  variable: '--font-inter',
})

const instrumentSerif = Instrument_Serif({
  subsets: ['latin'],
  weight: ['400'],
  style: ['normal', 'italic'],
  display: 'swap',
  variable: '--font-instrument-serif',
})
```

In the layout body:
```tsx
<html lang="en" className={`${inter.variable} ${instrumentSerif.variable}`}>
  <body className="font-body antialiased">
    {children}
  </body>
</html>
```

**How the font chain works**: `next/font` injects `--font-inter` and `--font-instrument-serif` as CSS custom properties on the `html` element. In `globals.css`, the `@theme` block defines `--font-display: var(--font-instrument-serif), ...` which Tailwind v4 picks up and exposes as `font-display`. When you write `font-display` on an element, it resolves through the chain: Tailwind utility → `--font-display` CSS var → `--font-instrument-serif` CSS var → the actual font stack. This is the correct v4 pattern — no `tailwind.config.js` override needed.

**Instrument Serif note**: This font is `Instrument_Serif` (underscore) in `next/font/google` imports, not `Instrument Serif`.

---

## 5. lib/motion.ts — Shared Animation Variants

```typescript
import type { Variants } from 'framer-motion'

export const fadeUp: Variants = {
  hidden: { opacity: 0, y: 18 },
  visible: {
    opacity: 1,
    y: 0,
    transition: { duration: 0.55, ease: [0.16, 1, 0.3, 1] },
  },
}

export const fadeIn: Variants = {
  hidden: { opacity: 0 },
  visible: {
    opacity: 1,
    transition: { duration: 0.45, ease: [0.16, 1, 0.3, 1] },
  },
}

export const staggerContainer: Variants = {
  hidden: {},
  visible: {
    transition: { staggerChildren: 0.08, delayChildren: 0.05 },
  },
}

export const staggerContainerSlow: Variants = {
  hidden: {},
  visible: {
    transition: { staggerChildren: 0.12, delayChildren: 0.1 },
  },
}

export const scaleIn: Variants = {
  hidden: { opacity: 0, scale: 0.97 },
  visible: {
    opacity: 1,
    scale: 1,
    transition: { duration: 0.6, ease: [0.16, 1, 0.3, 1] },
  },
}

// Standard whileInView props — use spread: {...inViewProps}
export const inViewProps = {
  initial: 'hidden',
  whileInView: 'visible',
  viewport: { once: true, amount: 0.2 },
} as const
```

---

## 6. FadeInView.tsx — Reusable Entrance Wrapper

```tsx
'use client'
import { motion } from 'framer-motion'
import { fadeUp, inViewProps } from '@/lib/motion'

export function FadeInView({
  children,
  delay = 0,
  className,
}: {
  children: React.ReactNode
  delay?: number
  className?: string
}) {
  return (
    <motion.div
      {...inViewProps}
      variants={fadeUp}
      transition={{ delay }}
      className={className}
    >
      {children}
    </motion.div>
  )
}
```

---

## 7. Component Architecture — Server vs Client

| Component | Directive | Reason |
|---|---|---|
| `Navbar` | `'use client'` | `useState` for scroll opacity and mobile drawer open/close |
| `HeroSection` | `'use client'` | `motion.div` with `useReducedMotion()` check, entrance animation on mount |
| `FadeInView` | `'use client'` | `motion.div` is a client component |
| `ProductPreviewSection` | `'use client'` | `useState` for active tab, `AnimatePresence` for cross-fade |
| `PainSection` | Server | Static HTML; uses `FadeInView` (client) as a child, which is fine |
| `FounderStoryBridgeSection` | Server | Static HTML; uses `FadeInView` |
| `WorkflowSection` | Server | Static content |
| `StacksSection` | Server | Static content |
| `ProofSection` | Server | Static content |
| `CustomSection` | Server | Static content |
| `FinalCTASection` | Server | Static content |
| `Footer` | Server | Static content |
| `Button` | Server | No interactivity beyond CSS hover |
| `Badge` | Server | Pure display |
| `Container` | Server | Layout wrapper only |

Server components are the default in Next.js App Router. Only components that require browser APIs, event handlers, or React hooks need `'use client'`. The key pattern: a server component CAN render a client component child — the client subtree takes over. So `PainSection` is a server component that renders `<FadeInView>` (client) children, which is the optimal pattern for reducing client-side JS.

---

## 8. Section-by-Section Implementation

### Section 1 — Navbar

**File**: `src/components/sections/Navbar.tsx`
**Directive**: `'use client'`

**Scroll behavior**: `useEffect` with a `window.addEventListener('scroll', ...)` listener. At 0px: `bg-transparent`. Past 40px: `bg-surface shadow-xs`. Transition: `transition-all duration-300`.

**Key classes**:
```
fixed top-0 left-0 right-0 z-50
px-6 md:px-10 lg:px-16
h-16 flex items-center justify-between
transition-all duration-300
```

Logo: `font-display text-[18px] text-text`

Nav links: `hidden md:flex gap-8` → each link: `text-sm text-text-muted hover:text-text transition-colors duration-200`

CTA button: `bg-accent text-white text-sm font-medium px-[18px] h-10 rounded-pill hover:bg-accent-hover transition-all duration-200 hover:-translate-y-0.5`

Mobile hamburger: `md:hidden` — renders SVG icon, `onClick` opens drawer state.

Mobile drawer: `fixed inset-0 z-40 bg-surface` — slides from right with framer-motion `x: '100%'` → `x: 0`, overlaid with a semi-transparent backdrop. Contains nav items stacked vertically with `text-xl font-display`.

## 9. ui/Button.tsx

```tsx
import { cn } from '@/lib/utils' // or inline

type ButtonVariant = 'primary' | 'secondary' | 'ghost'

interface ButtonProps extends React.ButtonHTMLAttributes<HTMLButtonElement> {
  variant?: ButtonVariant
  size?: 'sm' | 'md' | 'lg'
  as?: 'button' | 'a'
  href?: string
}
```

Classes per variant:
- primary: `bg-accent text-white hover:bg-accent-hover hover:-translate-y-0.5 shadow-xs`
- secondary: `bg-surface border border-border text-text hover:bg-surface-muted hover:-translate-y-0.5`
- ghost: `text-text-muted hover:text-text hover:bg-surface-muted`

Shared: `inline-flex items-center justify-center gap-2 rounded-pill font-medium transition-all duration-200 cursor-pointer`

Sizes: sm `h-9 px-4 text-[13px]`, md `h-11 px-5 text-sm`, lg `h-12 px-7 text-[15px]`

## 10. Mobile Responsive Key Behaviors

### Breakpoint system
- `sm`: 640px (not used heavily — skip straight to `md`)
- `md`: 768px — most grids switch from 1-col to multi-col
- `lg`: 1024px — hero splits, asymmetric layouts activate
- `xl`: 1280px — max container widths

### Hero: `< lg`
- Single column, visual below text
- H1 reduces via `clamp()` naturally
- CTA row stacks to `flex-col` at `< sm` if buttons are long
- Product frame: `aspect-[4/3]` not fixed height

### Navbar: `< md`
- Hide center nav links (`hidden md:flex`)
- Show hamburger `md:hidden`
- Drawer full-screen from right

### ProductPreview: All sizes
- Tab bar: `overflow-x-auto` with `flex-nowrap`
- Frame: `h-[280px]` mobile vs `h-[560px]` desktop
- Caption bar always visible (not hidden on mobile)
- Active tab scrolled into view via `ref.scrollIntoView({ behavior: 'smooth', inline: 'center' })`

### PainSection pain strip: `< md`
- `flex-nowrap overflow-x-auto` instead of `flex-wrap`
- Horizontal scrollable row with hidden scrollbar

### FounderStoryBridge collage: `< lg`
- Collage rendered as a simple grid of 2 cards instead of the layered absolute positioning
- `grid grid-cols-2 gap-3` for the two smaller cards
- Product frame above at full width

### StacksSection: `< md`
- Single column stack, featured card loses the `-mt-4` elevation (flat stack)
- Featured badge still shows

### ProofSection collage: `< lg`
- Stacked cards converted to a simple list of 2 cards in a column (no overlap/rotation)

### FinalCTA: All sizes
- H2 already uses `clamp()` so responsive by nature
- Padding: `py-24 md:py-32`

}
```

`page.tsx` stays a server component. All client interactivity is isolated in the child components that declare `'use client'`.

---

## 12. Motion Choreography Summary

| Section | Pattern | Trigger |
|---|---|---|
| Navbar | CSS `transition-all` on scroll | JS scroll listener |
| Hero | `initial/animate` stagger (not whileInView) | On mount |
| Hero product frame | `scaleIn` variant, 320ms delay | On mount |
| PainSection | `whileInView staggerContainer` → H2, body, strip | Scroll enter |
| FounderStoryBridge | Left: `fadeUp whileInView`. Right: `fadeUp` 120ms delay | Scroll enter |
| WorkflowSection | `staggerContainer` over 3 cards, 120ms stagger | Scroll enter |
| StacksSection | `staggerContainer` over 3 cards, 100ms stagger | Scroll enter |
| ProofSection | Left: `fadeUp`. Right collage: `fadeUp` delayed | Scroll enter |
| CustomSection | H2+body first, then tags `staggerContainer` 40ms | Scroll enter |
| FinalCTASection | H2+body `fadeUp`, then CTA 80ms later | Scroll enter |
| ProductPreview tab switch | `AnimatePresence mode="wait"` opacity 0→1, 220ms | Tab click |
| Mobile drawer | `framer-motion x: '100%' → 0`, `ease-standard` | Hamburger click |

**All `whileInView`** use `viewport={{ once: true, amount: 0.2 }}` to prevent re-animation on scroll-back.

**`prefers-reduced-motion`**: The CSS block in globals.css handles this at the CSS level. For framer-motion, use `useReducedMotion()` in the two client components that run on mount (Navbar, HeroSection) and short-circuit the animation values when true.

---

## 13. Tailwind v4 Specifics vs v3 — Critical Differences

1. **No `tailwind.config.js`**: All token definitions live in `@theme {}` inside globals.css. No `extend.colors`, no `extend.borderRadius`, etc.

2. **`--color-*` naming is mandatory**: To get `bg-accent`, you MUST define `--color-accent`. Defining `--accent` alone does NOT create a Tailwind utility — it becomes a CSS variable only. This is the most common mistake when migrating.

3. **`@theme` vs `:root`**: Design tokens used ONLY as CSS variables (not needing Tailwind utilities) can stay in `:root`. Tokens that need to generate utilities MUST be in `@theme`. The shadows, easing values, and duration values should stay in `:root` because Tailwind v4 has its own shadow scale — don't fight it. Use `shadow-[var(--shadow-md)]` as an escape hatch, or redefine `--shadow-*` in `@theme` to override Tailwind's defaults.

4. **`--radius-pill: 9999px`** maps to `rounded-pill`. In v3 you'd write `rounded-full`. In v4, since you're defining your own radius token, `rounded-pill` is the correct class.

5. **`--font-display` and `--font-body`**: In `@theme`, these create `font-display` and `font-body` utility classes that apply `font-family`. This is correct v4 behavior — no need for `fontFamily` in a config object.

6. **Arbitrary values still work**: `text-[clamp(2rem,5vw,5.5rem)]`, `bg-[rgba(248,247,244,0.7)]`, `h-[540px]` all work identically to v3.

7. **No `bg-opacity-*` utilities**: Tailwind v4 uses `bg-accent/50` syntax (slash opacity) natively. Same as v3's modern syntax.

8. **`border-border`**: Since you defined `--color-border`, you get `border-border` automatically. You do NOT need `border-[var(--color-border)]`.

---

## 14. Implementation Sequence

1. Run `npm install framer-motion@12.38.0`
2. Write `src/app/globals.css` with complete `@theme` block
3. Write `src/app/layout.tsx` with font imports and CSS variables applied to `html`
4. Create `src/lib/motion.ts` with shared variants
5. Create `src/components/ui/` primitives: `FadeInView.tsx`, `Button.tsx`, `Badge.tsx`, `Container.tsx`
6. Build sections in page order — `Navbar` first (always visible), then top-to-bottom
7. Wire everything in `src/app/page.tsx`
8. Test mobile at 390px (iPhone), 768px (tablet), 1440px (wide desktop)

---

- `/Users/juliuscecilia/Desktop/schoolstack/schoolstack_landing/src/app/globals.css`

Framer-motion is not yet installed. --- ## Step 0 — Install Framer Motion ```bash npm install framer-motion ``` Framer-motion v12+ supports React 19. Types are bundled — no `@types` package needed. --- ## File Structure ``` src/ ├── app/ │ ├── globals.css ← @theme design tokens + base styles │ ├── layout.tsx ← Instrument Serif + Inter via next/font/google │ └── page.tsx ← composes all sections │ ├── components/ │ ├── ui/ │ │ ├── FadeInView.tsx ← 'use client' — reusable framer-motion entrance wrapper │ │ ├── Button.tsx ← primary / secondary / ghost variants │ │ ├── Badge.tsx ← eyebrow chips │ │ └── Container.tsx ← max-width wrapper │ │ │ └── sections/ │ ├── Navbar.tsx ← 'use client' — scroll state + mobile drawer │ ├── HeroSection.tsx ← 'use client' — mount animations │ ├── PainSection.tsx ← server — uses FadeInView children │ ├── FounderStoryBridgeSection.tsx ← server │ ├── ProductPreviewSection.tsx ← 'use client' — tab state + AnimatePresence │ ├── WorkflowSection.tsx ← server │ ├── StacksSection.tsx ← server │ ├── ProofSection.tsx ← server │ ├── CustomSection.tsx ← server │ ├── FinalCTASection.tsx ← server │ └── Footer.tsx ← server │ └── lib/ └── motion.ts ← shared framer-motion variants + inViewProps ``` --- ## globals.css Tailwind v4 maps `@theme` tokens to utility classes. Others (radius, shadow, font) need their own namespace. ```css @import "tailwindcss"; @theme { /* Colors — accessible as bg-accent, text-text-muted, border-border, etc. */ --color-bg: #f6f5f2; --color-bg-alt: #f1efe9; --color-surface: #ffffff; --color-surface-soft: #fbfaf7; --color-surface-muted: #f3f1ec; --color-border: #e7e3dc; --color-border-strong: #d9d4cc; --color-text: #1f1e1a; --color-text-muted: #6f6b65; --color-text-faint: #9a958d; --color-accent: #244b46; --color-accent-hover: #1d3d39; --color-accent-soft: #dfe8e5; /* Radius — accessible as rounded-sm, rounded-md, rounded-lg, rounded-xl, rounded-pill */ --radius-sm: 10px; --radius-md: 14px; --radius-lg: 20px; --radius-xl: 28px; --radius-pill: 9999px; /* Shadows — accessible as shadow-xs, shadow-sm, shadow-md, shadow-lg */ --shadow-xs: 0 1px 2px rgba(20,20,20,0.04); --shadow-sm: 0 6px 24px rgba(20,20,20,0.05); --shadow-md: 0 14px 40px rgba(20,20,20,0.08); --shadow-lg: 0 24px 60px rgba(20,20,20,0.10); /* Fonts — accessible as font-display, font-body */ /* These reference the CSS vars injected by next/font/google in layout.tsx */ --font-display: var(--font-instrument-serif), "Instrument Serif", serif; --font-body: var(--font-inter), "Inter", sans-serif; } html { scroll-behavior: smooth; } body { background-color: var(--color-bg); color: var(--color-text); font-family: var(--font-body); -webkit-font-smoothing: antialiased; } h1, h2, h3 { font-family: var(--font-display); letter-spacing: -0.03em; line-height: 0.98; } @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { *, *::before, *::after { animation-duration: 0.01ms !important; transition-duration: 0.01ms !important; } } ``` --- ## layout.tsx — Font Setup `Instrument_Serif` (underscore, not space) from `next/font/google`. Stagger eyebrow→H1→body→CTAs at 80ms intervals - Product frame: `h-[540px] rounded-xl bg-surface border border-border shadow-lg overflow-hidden` - Placeholder hero visual: sidebar nav + admin kanban layout (see Product Preview Admin tab spec below) - Product frame uses `scaleIn` variant (scale 0.97→1, 320ms delay) ### PainSection (server) - `bg-bg-alt py-24`, narrow `max-w-[680px] mx-auto text-center` - H2: Instrument Serif `clamp(2rem,4vw,3rem)` - Pain strip: `flex-wrap justify-center gap-2 mt-10`; on mobile `flex-nowrap overflow-x-auto` - Each tool pill: `rounded-pill bg-surface-muted border border-border text-xs text-text-faint px-3 py-1.5` - Tools: Google Forms · Venmo · Gmail · Excel · Wix · Dropbox · SignNow · PDFs · Stripe · Google Docs - Animation: `staggerContainer` wrapping H2, body, strip — `whileInView once:true` ### FounderStoryBridgeSection (server) - 2-col asymmetric `grid-cols-1 lg:grid-cols-[1fr_1fr] gap-20 items-center` - Left: eyebrow, H2 `clamp(1.9rem,3.5vw,2.6rem)`, body, 4 bullet points with accent dot icons - Right: editorial collage using `relative h-[480px]` - Product crop frame: `absolute inset-0 right-12 bottom-20 rounded-xl bg-surface border shadow-md` - Founder note card: `absolute bottom-16 right-0 w-[220px] bg-surface border rounded-lg p-4 shadow-md`; quote in `font-display italic` - School facts card: `absolute bottom-0 left-4 w-[200px] bg-accent-soft border border-accent/20 rounded-lg p-4` - On `< lg`: collage converts to stacked 2-card grid, product frame above - Animation: left `fadeUp whileInView`, right collage 120ms delay ### ProductPreviewSection (`'use client'`) - `useState` for active tab; default: `'admin'` - Tab bar: `bg-surface border border-border rounded-pill shadow-xs p-1.5 flex gap-1 overflow-x-auto` - Active tab: `bg-accent text-white rounded-pill`; inactive: `text-text-muted rounded-pill hover:bg-surface-muted` - Keyboard: left/right arrows cycle tabs; `useRef` for scroll-into-view on mobile - Frame: `h-[560px] rounded-xl border border-border-strong shadow-lg overflow-hidden relative`; mobile `h-[280px]` - `AnimatePresence mode="wait"` → each panel `motion.div key={activeTab}` opacity 0→1 220ms - Caption bar: `absolute bottom-0 inset-x-0 px-5 py-3.5 bg-[rgba(15,14,12,0.75)] backdrop-blur-sm` #### Tab mockup compositions (HTML/CSS placeholder UIs): **Admin (default)** — Kanban pipeline: `flex h-full` → sidebar 150px (nav items: Dashboard, Applications●, Students, Parents, Teachers, Payments, Leads) + main with 3-column kanban (New Inquiry / Reviewing / Enrolled). Critical naming rule: only `--color-*` keys auto-generate `bg-*`, `text-*`, `border-*` utilities. Others (radius, shadow, font) need their own namespace. ```css @import "tailwindcss"; @theme { /* Colors — accessible as bg-accent, text-text-muted, border-border, etc. */ --color-bg: #f6f5f2; --color-bg-alt: #f1efe9; --color-surface: #ffffff; --color-surface-soft: #fbfaf7; --color-surface-muted: #f3f1ec; --color-border: #e7e3dc; --color-border-strong: #d9d4cc; --color-text: #1f1e1a; --color-text-muted: #6f6b65; --color-text-faint: #9a958d; --color-accent: #244b46; --color-accent-hover: #1d3d39; --color-accent-soft: #dfe8e5; /* Radius — accessible as rounded-sm, rounded-md, rounded-lg, rounded-xl, rounded-pill */ --radius-sm: 10px; --radius-md: 14px; --radius-lg: 20px; --radius-xl: 28px; --radius-pill: 9999px; /* Shadows — accessible as shadow-xs, shadow-sm, shadow-md, shadow-lg */ --shadow-xs: 0 1px 2px rgba(20,20,20,0.04); --shadow-sm: 0 6px 24px rgba(20,20,20,0.05); --shadow-md: 0 14px 40px rgba(20,20,20,0.08); --shadow-lg: 0 24px 60px rgba(20,20,20,0.10); /* Fonts — accessible as font-display, font-body */ /* These reference the CSS vars injected by next/font/google in layout.tsx */ --font-display: var(--font-instrument-serif), "Instrument Serif", serif; --font-body: var(--font-inter), "Inter", sans-serif; } html { scroll-behavior: smooth; } body { background-color: var(--color-bg); color: var(--color-text); font-family: var(--font-body); -webkit-font-smoothing: antialiased; } h1, h2, h3 { font-family: var(--font-display); letter-spacing: -0.03em; line-height: 0.98; } @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { *, *::before, *::after { animation-duration: 0.01ms !important; transition-duration: 0.01ms !important; } } ``` --- ## layout.tsx — Font Setup `Instrument_Serif` (underscore, not space) from `next/font/google`. In v3 you'd use `rounded-full`. 4. **`font-display`** works because `--font-display` is in `@theme`. 5. **Arbitrary values** work identically: `text-[clamp(...)]`, `bg-[rgba(...)]`, `h-[540px]`. 6. **`border-border`**: just works — no `border-[var(--color-border)]` needed. 7. **Opacity modifier**: `bg-accent/50` — v4 slash syntax works natively. --- ## Motion Summary | Section | Trigger | Pattern | |---|---|---| | Navbar | scroll | CSS transition (no framer) | | Hero | mount | `initial/animate` stagger, not `whileInView` | | Pain | scroll | `staggerContainer whileInView` | | FounderBridge | scroll | left `fadeUp`, right +120ms delay | | Workflow steps | scroll | `staggerContainerSlow` 120ms | | Stack cards | scroll | `staggerContainer` 100ms | | Proof | scroll | left `fadeUp`, right collage +120ms | | Custom tags | scroll | `staggerContainer` 40ms | | FinalCTA | scroll | `fadeUp` then CTA +80ms | | Product tab switch | click | `AnimatePresence mode="wait"` opacity 220ms | | Mobile drawer | click | `x: '100%' → 0` ease-standard | All `whileInView` use `viewport={{ once: true, amount: 0.2 }}`. --- ## Next.js 16 / React 19 Notes - All async request APIs (`cookies`, `headers`, `params`) must be awaited — not relevant for this static marketing page - `'use client'` components: Navbar, HeroSection, FadeInView, ProductPreviewSection - All other sections are server components (React defaults) - `Instrument_Serif` (underscore) is the correct `next/font/google` import name --- ## Verification 1. `npm run dev` — page renders at localhost:3000 2. ` in Hero, `

` in each section

Start with updating your todo list if applicable Your plan has been saved to: .claude-account3/plans/lets-create-our-landing-resilient-garden.md You can refer back to it if needed during implementation. ## Approved Plan: # School Stack Landing Page — Implementation Plan ## Context The School Stack marketing site is currently a blank canvas (`src/app/page.tsx` has only `

schoolstack

`). The goal is to build the complete landing page per the revised spec — a high-fidelity, conversion-focused marketing page that combines the Eden-inspired design system with School Stack's founder-origin narrative. The page should feel like a premium creative software experience, not a generic SaaS template. Stack: **Next.js 16.2.4 · React 19 · TypeScript · Tailwind CSS 4.2.2**. Framer-motion is not yet installed. --- ## Step 0 — Install Framer Motion ```bash npm install framer-motion ``` Framer-motion v12+ supports React 19. Types are bundled — no `@types` package needed. --- ## File Structure ``` src/ ├── app/ │ ├── globals.css ← @theme design tokens + base styles │ ├── layout.tsx ← Instrument Serif + Inter via next/font/google │ └── page.tsx ← composes all sections │ ├── components/ │ ├── ui/ │ │ ├── FadeInView.tsx ← 'use client' — reusable framer-motion entrance wrapper │ │ ├── Button.tsx ← primary / secondary / ghost variants │ │ ├── Badge.tsx ← eyebrow chips │ │ └── Container.tsx ← max-width wrapper │ │ │ └── sections/ │ ├── Navbar.tsx ← 'use client' — scroll state + mobile drawer │ ├── HeroSection.tsx ← 'use client' — mount animations │ ├── PainSection.tsx ← server — uses FadeInView children │ ├── FounderStoryBridgeSection.tsx ← server │ ├── ProductPreviewSection.tsx ← 'use client' — tab state + AnimatePresence │ ├── WorkflowSection.tsx ← server │ ├── StacksSection.tsx ← server │ ├── ProofSection.tsx ← server │ ├── CustomSection.tsx ← server │ ├── FinalCTASection.tsx ← server │ └── Footer.tsx ← server │ └── lib/ └── motion.ts ← shared framer-motion variants + inViewProps ``` --- ## globals.css Tailwind v4 maps `@theme` tokens to utility classes. Critical naming rule: only `--color-*` keys auto-generate `bg-*`, `text-*`, `border-*` utilities. Others (radius, shadow, font) need their own namespace. ```css @import "tailwindcss"; @theme { /* Colors — accessible as bg-accent, text-text-muted, border-border, etc. */ --color-bg: #f6f5f2; --color-bg-alt: #f1efe9; --color-surface: #ffffff; --color-surface-soft: #fbfaf7; --color-surface-muted: #f3f1ec; --color-border: #e7e3dc; --color-border-strong: #d9d4cc; --color-text: #1f1e1a; --color-text-muted: #6f6b65; --color-text-faint: #9a958d; --color-accent: #244b46; --color-accent-hover: #1d3d39; --color-accent-soft: #dfe8e5; /* Radius — accessible as rounded-sm, rounded-md, rounded-lg, rounded-xl, rounded-pill */ --radius-sm: 10px; --radius-md: 14px; --radius-lg: 20px; --radius-xl: 28px; --radius-pill: 9999px; /* Shadows — accessible as shadow-xs, shadow-sm, shadow-md, shadow-lg */ --shadow-xs: 0 1px 2px rgba(20,20,20,0.04); --shadow-sm: 0 6px 24px rgba(20,20,20,0.05); --shadow-md: 0 14px 40px rgba(20,20,20,0.08); --shadow-lg: 0 24px 60px rgba(20,20,20,0.10); /* Fonts — accessible as font-display, font-body */ /* These reference the CSS vars injected by next/font/google in layout.tsx */ --font-display: var(--font-instrument-serif), "Instrument Serif", serif; --font-body: var(--font-inter), "Inter", sans-serif; } html { scroll-behavior: smooth; } body { background-color: var(--color-bg); color: var(--color-text); font-family: var(--font-body); -webkit-font-smoothing: antialiased; } h1, h2, h3 { font-family: var(--font-display); letter-spacing: -0.03em; line-height: 0.98; } @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { *, *::before, *::after { animation-duration: 0.01ms !important; transition-duration: 0.01ms !important; } } ``` --- ## layout.tsx — Font Setup `Instrument_Serif` (underscore, not space) from `next/font/google`. Weight `'400'` only — this font has no bold. Both fonts use `variable` mode so their CSS custom properties get injected on ``. ```tsx import { Inter, Instrument_Serif } from 'next/font/google' const inter = Inter({ subsets: ['latin'], display: 'swap', variable: '--font-inter', }) const instrumentSerif = Instrument_Serif({ subsets: ['latin'], weight: ['400'], style: ['normal', 'italic'], display: 'swap', variable: '--font-instrument-serif', }) // Apply both variable classes to // globals.css @theme picks up --font-instrument-serif and --font-inter ``` --- ## lib/motion.ts Shared variants used across all sections: - `fadeUp` — y: 18 → 0, opacity 0 → 1, 550ms ease-standard - `fadeIn` — opacity only, 450ms - `scaleIn` — scale 0.97 → 1 + fade, 600ms (hero product frame) - `staggerContainer` — 80ms stagger between children - `staggerContainerSlow` — 120ms stagger (workflow steps, stack cards) - `inViewProps` — `{ initial: 'hidden', whileInView: 'visible', viewport: { once: true, amount: 0.2 } }` --- ## Section Implementations ### Navbar (`'use client'`) - `useEffect` scroll listener: transparent at 0px, `bg-surface shadow-xs` past 40px - `useState` for mobile drawer (open/close) - Logo: `font-display text-[18px] text-text` - Nav links: `hidden md:flex gap-8` — `text-sm text-text-muted hover:text-text` - CTA: `bg-accent text-white rounded-pill px-[18px] h-10 text-sm hover:bg-accent-hover hover:-translate-y-0.5` - Mobile drawer: framer-motion `x: '100%' → 0`, slides from right, full-screen ### HeroSection (`'use client'`) - Grid: `grid-cols-1 lg:grid-cols-[45fr_55fr]`, `pt-[140px] pb-[120px]` - Eyebrow chip, H1 `clamp(2.75rem,5.5vw,5.25rem)`, body `max-w-[52ch]`, CTA row - Animations: `initial/animate` (above fold, not `whileInView`). Stagger eyebrow→H1→body→CTAs at 80ms intervals - Product frame: `h-[540px] rounded-xl bg-surface border border-border shadow-lg overflow-hidden` - Placeholder hero visual: sidebar nav + admin kanban layout (see Product Preview Admin tab spec below) - Product frame uses `scaleIn` variant (scale 0.97→1, 320ms delay) ### PainSection (server) - `bg-bg-alt py-24`, narrow `max-w-[680px] mx-auto text-center` - H2: Instrument Serif `clamp(2rem,4vw,3rem)` - Pain strip: `flex-wrap justify-center gap-2 mt-10`; on mobile `flex-nowrap overflow-x-auto` - Each tool pill: `rounded-pill bg-surface-muted border border-border text-xs text-text-faint px-3 py-1.5` - Tools: Google Forms · Venmo · Gmail · Excel · Wix · Dropbox · SignNow · PDFs · Stripe · Google Docs - Animation: `staggerContainer` wrapping H2, body, strip — `whileInView once:true` ### FounderStoryBridgeSection (server) - 2-col asymmetric `grid-cols-1 lg:grid-cols-[1fr_1fr] gap-20 items-center` - Left: eyebrow, H2 `clamp(1.9rem,3.5vw,2.6rem)`, body, 4 bullet points with accent dot icons - Right: editorial collage using `relative h-[480px]` - Product crop frame: `absolute inset-0 right-12 bottom-20 rounded-xl bg-surface border shadow-md` - Founder note card: `absolute bottom-16 right-0 w-[220px] bg-surface border rounded-lg p-4 shadow-md`; quote in `font-display italic` - School facts card: `absolute bottom-0 left-4 w-[200px] bg-accent-soft border border-accent/20 rounded-lg p-4` - On `< lg`: collage converts to stacked 2-card grid, product frame above - Animation: left `fadeUp whileInView`, right collage 120ms delay ### ProductPreviewSection (`'use client'`) - `useState` for active tab; default: `'admin'` - Tab bar: `bg-surface border border-border rounded-pill shadow-xs p-1.5 flex gap-1 overflow-x-auto` - Active tab: `bg-accent text-white rounded-pill`; inactive: `text-text-muted rounded-pill hover:bg-surface-muted` - Keyboard: left/right arrows cycle tabs; `useRef` for scroll-into-view on mobile - Frame: `h-[560px] rounded-xl border border-border-strong shadow-lg overflow-hidden relative`; mobile `h-[280px]` - `AnimatePresence mode="wait"` → each panel `motion.div key={activeTab}` opacity 0→1 220ms - Caption bar: `absolute bottom-0 inset-x-0 px-5 py-3.5 bg-[rgba(15,14,12,0.75)] backdrop-blur-sm` #### Tab mockup compositions (HTML/CSS placeholder UIs): **Admin (default)** — Kanban pipeline: `flex h-full` → sidebar 150px (nav items: Dashboard, Applications●, Students, Parents, Teachers, Payments, Leads) + main with 3-column kanban (New Inquiry / Reviewing / Enrolled). Application cards show student name, program badge, date. Column headers with count badges. **Enrollment** — Multi-step form: Centered form card `max-w-[480px] rounded-xl border p-8`. Step progress indicator (steps 1-5, step 2 active in accent). Section: "Parent & Guardian Information". Fields: First Name / Last Name grid, Email, Phone, Relationship dropdown. Back + Continue buttons. **Website** — School site preview: Stacked layout: top nav bar with "Sage Field" logo + nav links + "Apply Now" pill. Hero band `bg-bg-alt` with H1 "Welcome to Sage Field" in `font-display`. Below: 3 program cards. **Parents** — Portal dashboard: Sidebar (avatar + nav: Dashboard●, Forms, Billing, Messages) + main with "Hello, Emma" greeting, enrollment checklist card (✓ Application, ✓ Health form, ○ Contract, ○ Tuition), billing preview card. **Teachers** — Student cards + notes: Sidebar (Students●, Notes, Hours, Feed) + main with 2×2 student card grid + note list below. Each card: student name, program badge, note count. Notes show category badge + excerpt. **Payments** — Transaction table: Table with columns: Student | Type | Amount | Date | Status. 5-6 rows. Status pills: `bg-green-50 text-green-700` for paid. Right sidebar showing selected transaction detail. **Leads** — CRM table: Header with "Leads CRM" + search + "New Lead" button. Status tabs (All 14 / New / Contacted / Touring / Enrolled). Table: Name | Interest | Status | Tags | Last Contact. Tag pills in `bg-accent-soft text-accent`. ### WorkflowSection (server) - `bg-bg-alt py-24`, centered heading - 3-card `grid grid-cols-1 md:grid-cols-3 gap-6 mt-16 relative` - Connecting line (desktop only): `absolute top-[52px] left-[calc(16.67%+24px)] right-[calc(16.67%+24px)] h-px bg-border pointer-events-none` - Step cards: `bg-surface border border-border rounded-lg p-8 shadow-xs` - Step number `text-[11px] font-medium text-text-faint uppercase tracking-widest` - Large decorative number `text-[3rem] font-display text-border-strong leading-none mt-1` - Title `text-[17px] font-medium text-text mt-3` - Body `text-sm text-text-muted mt-2 leading-relaxed` - `staggerContainerSlow` wrapping 3 cards, `whileInView once:true` ### StacksSection (server) - `bg-bg py-24`, centered heading (no pricing shown, CTA-only) - Grid: `grid grid-cols-1 md:grid-cols-3 gap-6 mt-12 items-start` - Standard card: `bg-surface border border-border rounded-xl p-8 shadow-xs` - Featured (Growth): `border-2 border-accent shadow-md md:-mt-4 relative` + `"Recommended"` badge `absolute -top-3.5 left-1/2 -translate-x-1/2 bg-accent text-white text-[11px] rounded-pill px-4 py-1` - Feature list bullets: `w-1.5 h-1.5 rounded-full bg-accent mt-1.5 flex-shrink-0` - CTAs: Featured gets `bg-accent text-white`; others get `border border-border text-text` - Tiers: Starter (solo, 20 students), Growth (3–5 teachers, 20–60 students), Academy (60+ students) - Below all cards: "Need something custom?" text link in accent ### ProofSection (server) - `bg-bg-alt py-24`, 2-col `grid-cols-1 lg:grid-cols-[5fr_7fr] gap-16 items-center` - Left: eyebrow, H2 "The founder story is the product story.", body, pull quote card, "See the school →" link - Pull quote card: `bg-surface-muted border border-border-strong rounded-lg p-6` — NO left border accent rail;
